mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior month lowers discount-rate pressure on equities and lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is directional only when the policy rate is already in an easing path; flat or rising rates do not support the mechanism.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a month-over-month FEDFUNDS decline, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE above a recession-style threshold signals deteriorating household income expectations and weaker risk appetite, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is expected to be strongest after a rapid unemployment increase rather than during stable unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the average forward return is not materially below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains depressed, consumer spending is strong but housing supply remains constrained, a combination that supports near-term equity revenues and prices. The interaction matters because robust sales alone are not sufficient if activity is broad-based weakness elsewhere.
regime_hint: consumer-led expansion with weak housing supply
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-HOUST interaction term does not improve out-of-sample sign prediction versus either series alone, or if positive signals fail to produce above-baseline 20-day returns in triggered interaction states.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO indicates weakening industrial activity and tighter earnings conditions for cyclicals, which should pull the broad index lower over the next 20 trading days. The signal should be strongest when output has already slowed for at least one month and not when production is rebounding.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or if the signal has no incremental predictive value after controlling for lagged market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in GDPC1 signals improving aggregate final demand and supports higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect should fail when GDP growth is revised away or when growth is driven by inventory noise rather than final demand.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not coincide with above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns over a rolling 3-year evaluation window, or if predictive power disappears after GDP revision updates are incorporated.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor-market deterioration implies weaker consumption and higher recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This is a joint condition because payroll deceleration without rising unemployment is too mild to trigger the equity response.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth turns negative and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE condition does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of activated observations, or if the interaction term is not significantly better than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration followed by a FEDFUNDS increase indicates tightening real-rate pressure and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is contingent on the policy rate responding upward after inflation has already firmed.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates and FEDFUNDS rises within 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-plus-hike episodes are not followed by negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the interaction coefficient is unstable across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising HOUST series after a prior trough signals a housing-cycle inflection that supports construction activity, consumer wealth effects, and broader demand, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The pattern fails when housing starts rise in a weak labor market with no accompanying stabilization in demand.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month housing-start upturns do not correspond to positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the effect vanishes when PAYEMS is not simultaneously stable or improving.
